cards.title: I wish my son wanted to spend more time with me
cards.body: A parent seeks advice about their adult son's limited contact, wondering if they're putting him under pressure. The guidance explores whether overcompensating for initial reluctance to have children might be affecting the relationship.

cards.title: Having children makes you smarter? Incredible, but true
cards.body: Research shows mothers and fathers have healthier, younger-looking brains than their childless peers. Years of mental and emotional gymnastics from parenting appear to provide cognitive benefits.

cards.title: I kept fearing the worst on our family holiday
cards.body: A parent describes struggling with anxiety while their children and partner seemed genuinely relaxed. A real emergency ultimately taught them they were more capable of handling crises than they believed.

cards.title: 5 Values That Reduce Anxiety
cards.body: A mother's wisdom about staying true to your values creates hope and reduces anxiety. Five values-based principles offer parents practical guidance for managing stress.

cards.title: The Dad Nobody Screens For
cards.body: A father's postpartum anxiety went undiagnosed while his wife received screening after a traumatic birth. His withdrawal during those critical months highlights the gap in mental health support for new fathers.

cards.title: A mayor in Japan announced maternity leave
cards.body: Shoko Kawata, 35, faced criticism for taking maternity leave but defended her decision proudly. The announcement sparked nationwide conversation about motherhood and professional responsibility.
